role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for selecting the home health agency and securing the start of care date, while the Patient Navigator audits demographic accuracy in Atlantis, educates the family on the Healing Partners program, and documents the family's anxiety regarding the coordination delays.
narrative_summary: Elena Rodriguez, a 74-year-old recovering from intensive chemotherapy and abdominal surgery, was eager to return home to her quiet apartment but faced significant anxiety as her discharge date approached without a confirmed home health provider. Although she was clinically cleared for discharge, the transition stalled because the primary home health agency had not yet assigned a nurse or confirmed a start of care window. The Patient Navigator acted as a supportive liaison by documenting the family's growing frustration in Atlantis and providing education on the initial medical assistant visit to help manage expectations during the bureaucratic delay.
demographic_audit_note: The Navigator performed a side-by-side audit of the facility face sheet against the Atlantis record and identified that the primary contact number was an inactive landline; the record was updated in Atlantis with the daughter's current mobile number to ensure the home team could maintain contact.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with specialized oncology recovery support and wound care.
v_card_flyer_status: During a bedside visit, the Navigator introduced the Healing Partners program and assisted the daughter in saving the V-Card to her contacts, explaining that this would allow them to identify our team's caller ID during the critical first 24 hours after discharge.
pre_dc_pulse_call: The Navigator conducted the 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call and found the family was prepared for the physical transition, though they remained highly concerned about the lack of a confirmed nurse arrival time from the home health agency.
atlantis_final_sync: After the Social Worker verbally confirmed that the agency had finally accepted the case, the Navigator recorded the discharge date and the 24-hour Medical Assistant visit window in Atlantis.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator met with the Social Worker who reported that the home health referral was sent but the agency had not yet responded; the Navigator documented this stall in Atlantis and informed the daughter that we were waiting for the agency's confirmation.

During a follow-up with the family, the daughter expressed frustration that they still did not have a nurse name or visit time; the Navigator documented this sentiment in Atlantis and asked the Social Worker for a verbal status update on the referral progress.

The Social Worker confirmed that the agency finally accepted the referral and assigned a nurse for the following afternoon; the Navigator updated the transition plan in Atlantis and provided the family with the Medical Assistant's expected arrival window.

format_2_triples.situation: The home health agency has not confirmed the referral after 48 hours.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator documented the communication gap in Atlantis and requested a verbal status update from the Social Worker.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter is anxious about what will happen on the first day home without a nurse.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator educated the family on the role of the Medical Assistant and the 24-hour post-discharge visit window to provide a concrete timeline.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's contact information in Atlantis does not match the facility face sheet.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator corrected the phone number and address in the Atlantis queue to prevent future routing errors.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notes in Atlantis that the Social Worker has not yet provided an update on the agency acceptance and chooses to wait for the next scheduled interdisciplinary meeting to ask for a status report.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the referral process and avoids unnecessary interruptions while maintaining an auditable trail of the delay in the Navigator's own records.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asks the Social Worker for a verbal update on the referral status and, upon hearing it is still pending, visits the patient to provide a program flyer and ensure the V-Card is saved for future caller ID recognition.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: The Navigator stays within their lane by gathering information from the Social Worker and focusing on family education and data accuracy without contacting outside vendors.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency's intake coordinator directly to advocate for Mrs. Rodriguez, offering to provide the clinical wound care notes if it will help the agency accept the referral faster.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the Navigator is prohibited from contacting home health vendors directly and must never handle or transmit clinical documentation.
